items.headline: PLD Athena Phase B receives $35M site work permit
items.summary: A $35 million site preparation project has been permitted at 5853 Rue Ferrari, making it by far the costliest permit issued this week. The scope suggests a substantial development, though permit details don't specify what will be built.

items.headline: Tous Les Jours bakery-café permitted at Evergreen Place
items.summary: A new Tous Les Jours location has received a permit at 2394 Evergreen Pl. The Korean bakery-café chain is expanding its Bay Area presence with this $476,000 buildout.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument artwork agreement approved
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust regarding the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by artist Mario Chiodo located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District budget set
items.summary: City Council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's FY 2026-27 budget and annual assessment.

items.headline: Changes proposed to City Council district office budgets
items.summary: City Council reviewed proposed changes to City Council Policy 1-18, Section 20, governing how Office of the Mayor and City Council District Office budgets are handled in transition years.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills the top administrative position responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing Council policy.

items.headline: City to consider 2026 community and neighborhood grant applications
items.summary: The City Council will review spring-cycle applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program on May 18. Residents can submit public comment on which projects should receive funding.

items.headline: Sustainability Commission reviews draft urban water master plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will discuss Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The plan addresses how the city manages water supply and responds to drought conditions.
